title: Mamba Selective State-Space Module
description: A sequence-mixing module that updates a compact recurrent state as each token arrives, using input-dependent parameters to decide what the state stores, forgets, and emits.
openingSummary: A selective state-space module (SSM) processes tokens in order while carrying a small hidden state forward through time. Mamba makes that state input-dependent so each step can selectively store, forget, and emit information instead of using fixed recurrence rules.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A state-space module is a sequence-mixing block that reads tokens one at a time and keeps a compact hidden state that summarizes what came before. At each position, the module combines the current token with that carried state to produce an output and an updated state for the next step. A selective state-space model (SSM) changes the update rules based on the current input rather than applying the same fixed transition at every position.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: Long sequences strain attention because dense attention compares every token to every other token. State-space modules offer a different path: they propagate information through a fixed-size state as the sequence unfolds, which can scale more gently with length when the recurrence is implemented efficiently.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: The module walks the sequence in order. For each token, it projects the input into input-dependent parameters that control the state transition and output. The previous hidden state is updated into a new state, and the module emits an output vector for that position. In Mamba, those transition parameters depend on the current token, so the block can decide what to remember, what to discard, and what to expose at each step. That selective behavior is the main change from earlier fixed-coefficient state-space designs.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: Dense attention forms outputs from pairwise token scores. A selective state-space step instead updates a hidden state and projects an output from that state. The formulas below contrast multi-head attention against a selective state update and output projection at one time step.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Multi-head attention mixes tokens by scoring every query against every key position. Linear attention keeps a running summary but still routes through attention-style feature maps. A Mamba-style state-space module does not build an explicit all-pairs score map; it compresses history into a recurrent state and lets input-dependent gates shape each update.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Hybrid stacks such as Nemotron 3 Super combine Mamba-style state-space layers with attention layers so the model can use efficient recurrence for much of the depth while keeping attention where direct token-to-token comparison is still useful.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: State-space mixing is inherently sequential: each step waits on the previous state, which affects parallel training patterns and can complicate implementation compared with batched attention kernels. The compact state also means less direct all-pairs token comparison than dense attention provides in a single layer, which is one reason hybrid Mamba-attention models still include attention blocks. None of this replaces benchmark evaluation; it describes architectural tradeoffs readers should expect when comparing sequence modules.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: Selective state-space modules remain a practical reference for long-context and hybrid designs because they show how recurrent state updates can carry sequence information with different cost and inductive bias than attention-only stacks.

computeFlow.title: Sequence mixing over time
computeFlow.alt: Dense multi-head attention versus Mamba selective state update over time
computeFlow.caption: Dense attention compares the current query against every past key position. Mamba instead walks tokens in order and updates a compact hidden state through input-dependent gates at each step.

mamba.sequenceMixing: Ordered recurrent state updates with input-dependent transition parameters
mamba.pairwiseComparison: No explicit n-by-n attention map; history flows through a compact state
mamba.stateCarry: Fixed-size hidden state carried across positions

mha.sequenceMixing: Softmax-weighted combination of value vectors from all key positions
mha.pairwiseComparison: Every query token scores against every key token
mha.stateCarry: Past tokens remain addressable through keys and values at each step

linear.sequenceMixing: Feature-map accumulation over keys without materializing full score matrix
linear.pairwiseComparison: Approximates dense attention through kernel-style summaries
linear.stateCarry: Running key-value summaries rather than a single recurrent state vector

ssmSchema.label: Selective state-space step
ssmSchema.formula: h_t = \bar{A}_t h_{t-1} + \bar{B}_t x_t, \quad y_t = C_t h_t

ht.definition: Hidden state after processing token at position t.

htm1.definition: Hidden state carried from the previous position.

xt.definition: Input vector for the token at position t.

yt.definition: Output vector emitted at position t.

at.definition: Input-dependent state transition applied at step t.

bt.definition: Input-dependent input injection at step t.

ct.definition: Input-dependent output projection at step t.
